title: Temporal Coarse-Graining as the Origin of Macroscopic Friction in Quantum Spin Chains via Data-Driven Liouvillian Extraction
abstract: Understanding the emergence of macroscopic irreversible hydrodynamics from the reversible unitary dynamics of isolated quantum many-body systems remains a fundamental challenge. Conventional approaches often force spin density dynamics into purely diffusive models, obscuring the microscopic interplay of pressure, spin current, and local friction. Furthermore, reconciling true irreversibility with strictly unitary evolution raises profound questions about the role of the observer's temporal resolution. In this paper, we introduce a fully data-driven framework based on generalized Extended Dynamic Mode Decomposition (gEDMD) integrated with the Mori-Zwanzig projection. By expanding the observable dictionary to explicitly include spin currents, we directly extract the Navier-Stokes hydrodynamic coefficients from a chaotic XXZ spin chain across varying temporal coarse-graining scales. Our unconstrained extraction reveals a profound physical dichotomy: the mechanical elasticity ($c^2$) is intrinsically derived from the exact unitary dynamics, preserving strict microscopic reversibility. In stark contrast, the macroscopic friction ($γ$) and kinematic viscosity ($ν$) exhibit zero net dissipation, oscillating rapidly around zero in the exact-derivative limit. We demonstrate that genuine macroscopic transport cannot be established without finite temporal coarse-graining. By introducing a finite observation timescale ($Δt_{\rm cg} > 0$), the system passes through a distinct crossover timescale where these reversible fluctuations average out, establishing an intermediate functional regime that yields strictly positive friction and viscosity. Our results clearly demonstrate that macroscopic friction in isolated quantum systems is not an absolute property, but fundamentally an emergent phenomenon dictated by the temporal resolution of the observer.
summary: This paper demonstrates that macroscopic friction and viscosity in isolated quantum systems are emergent phenomena resulting from temporal coarse-graining. By using a data-driven gEDMD approach on a chaotic XXZ spin chain, the author shows that while microscopic dynamics remain reversible, a finite observation timescale averages out fluctuations to establish a steady, dissipative fluid regime.
main_problem: How macroscopic irreversible hydrodynamics, such as friction and viscosity, emerge from the reversible, unitary dynamics of isolated quantum many-body systems.
main_result: Macroscopic friction and viscosity are not intrinsic microscopic properties but emerge through temporal coarse-gasting, which averages out reversible microscopic fluctuations.
method: A data-driven framework combining exact-derivative generalized Extended Dynamic Mode Decomposition (gEDMD) with the Mori-Zwanzig projection formalism.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional, chaotic XXZ spin-1/2 quantum spin chain with nearest-neighbor and next-nearest-neighbor interactions to break integrability.
key_observables: Local spin density, spin currents, and higher-order correlations (up to 3-site densities).
important_parameters: Temporal coarse-graining scale (delta t_cg), mechanical elasticity (c^2), macroscopic friction (gamma), and kinematic viscosity (nu).
main_assumptions: The use of a restricted dictionary of observables effectively acts as a spatial projection, and the system is assumed to be in a chaotic, thermalizing regime.
figures_summary: Figure 2 shows the Liouvillian spectrum dependence on dictionary completeness; Figure 3 compares exact-derivative vs. finite-difference gEDMD; Figure 4 shows predictive accuracy of trajectories; Figure 8 shows the time evolution of hydrodynamic coefficients; Figure 9 plots coefficients against the coarse-graining scale.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of emergent hydrodynamics, presents the gEDMD/Mori-Zwanzig framework, validates the method using dictionary completeness, demonstrates the role of temporal resolution in establishing friction, and analyzes the limits of the fluid model.
why_interesting: It provides a fundamental link between unitary quantum evolution and classical-like dissipative hydrodynamics, specifically identifying the observer's temporal resolution as the mechanism for the emergence of irreversibility.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Data-driven extraction framework and the physical dichotomy of temporal coarse-graining. (a) Observable dictionary explicitly incorporating both the local spin density Zi and the spin current Ji as relevant variables extracted from the full Hilbert space via Galerkin projection. (b) Construction of the localized data matrix Xwin(t) by ex- tracting discrete snapshots at intervals dt over a sliding time window τ. (c) The two analytical paradigms of the gEDMD framework. The localized data matrix is processed using ei- ther an exact continuous time derivative (∆tcg →0) to pre- serve strict microscopic reversibility, or a finite-difference tem- poral coarse-graining (∆tcg > 0) to capture...

FIG. 2. Liouvillian spectrum dependence on dictio- nary completeness. (a) When using a restricted macro- scopic dictionary (Dict A), eigenvalues scatter across the com- plex plane (Re(λ) ̸= 0). The emergence of negative real parts reflects apparent dissipation due to information leaking into unobserved microscopic degrees of freedom, while positive real parts indicate apparent amplification driven by finite-size quantum echoes. (b) When utilizing the complete Pauli basis (Dict B), all eigenvalues align perfectly on the imaginary axis (Re(λ) = 0), recovering the strict reversibility of the isolated quantum system. FIG. 4. Predictive capability. (a) Trajectory of the macroscopic bulk spin density ⟨Z3(t)⟩computed via exact Schr¨odinger dynamics (solid gray line), compared against the analytical reconstructions eLtX(0) using the macroscopic generator LA (Dict A, dashed blue line) and the complete generator LB (Dict B, dotted red line). (b) Absolute recon- struction error for both dictionaries. The complete dictionary (Dict B) perfectly captures complex Poincar´e revivals without artificial damping across 2,000 steps, maintaining an absolute error strictly bounded within 10−3. Conversely, the macro- scopic dictionary (Dict A) deviates, visually demonstrating the breakdown of the Markovian approximation...

FIG. 3. The physical dichotomy of temporal resolu- tion. (a) Applying finite time differences (∆tcg > 0) causes the eigenvalues to spread into the dissipative left-half plane, illustrating the physical emergence of Markovian dissipation through temporal coarse-graining. (b) Our Exact-Derivative gEDMD completely preserves the mathematical purity of the isolated system, ensuring the spectrum remains strictly re- versible (Re(λ) = 0), a necessary limit for deriving the me- chanical elasticity (c2).

FIG. 5. Genesis of decoherence in an 8-qubit quench (tq = 10.0). The left column shows the spectra obtained us- ing the complete basis of all 65, 535 observables, while the right column shows the spectra extracted using the restricted target dictionary (Dict S). The exact system’s eigenvalues re- main strictly on the imaginary axis (Re(λ) = 0), demonstrat- ing absolute unitarity. In contrast, the spectrum of Dict S scatters across the complex plane (Re(λ) ̸= 0) after contact, visualizing both the rapid loss of local information to the bath (negative real parts) and the subsequent quantum echoes from the finite environment (positive real parts).
